title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {gp3bayes: Contract-First Bayesian Workflows for Hierarchical Behavioural Data},   author = {Stefanos Balaskas},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://stefanosbalaskas.github.io/gp3bayes/}, }

title: gp3bayes
text: gp3bayes independent R package development transparent, contract-first Bayesian workflows repeated-measures hierarchical behavioural data.

title: gp3bayes
text: package currently provides: explicit model contracts; model-readiness audits; deterministic hierarchical binary simulation recorded truth; explicit binary-outcome condition transformations; inspectable backend-independent prior specifications; restricted hierarchical formula construction; structured prior-predictive plausibility checks; restricted translation approved binary specifications brms; optional full-MCMC binary fitting fixed brms rstan route. initial development scope restricted : hierarchical Bernoulli-logit models binary trial-level outcomes; hierarchical lognormal models strictly positive uncensored durations. Core contract, validation, simulation, preparation, specification, prior-predictive functionality require Gazepoint hardware, Gazepoint exports, gp3tools, proprietary software, private data, Bayesian backend. Binary fitting requires optional brms rstan packages.

title: gp3bayes
text: create_model_contract() records approved methodological specification neutral column mappings one initial model family. Creating contract validate data, fit model, establish model adequacy.
code: binary_contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "binary",   outcome_col = "selected",   participant_col = "participant_id",   item_col = "stimulus_id",   trial_col = "trial_id",   condition_col = "condition" )  binary_contract ## <gp3bayes_model_contract> ##   Family: binary ##   Likelihood: Bernoulli ##   Link: logit ##   Outcome: selected ##   Participant: participant_id ##   Item: stimulus_id ##   Condition: condition ##   Random slope requested: FALSE ##   Fitting performed: FALSE

title: gp3bayes
text: audit_model_readiness() evaluates observable data requirements formula construction model fitting. Failures block progression, whereas warnings identify structures requiring review.
code: binary_data <- data.frame(   participant_id = rep(c("p1", "p2"), each = 4),   stimulus_id = rep(paste0("s", 1:4), times = 2),   trial_id = rep(1:4, times = 2),   condition = rep(c("control", "treatment"), times = 4),   selected = c(0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 1, 0) )  readiness_audit <- audit_model_readiness(   binary_data,   binary_contract )  readiness_audit ## <gp3bayes_readiness_audit> ##   Family: binary ##   Rows: 8 ##   Status: ready ##   Ready: TRUE ##   Checks: 18 passed, 0 warnings, 0 failures

title: gp3bayes
text: build_model_formula() translates approved contract R formula, create_prior_specification() records family-appropriate priors without creating backend-specific objects. ready audit, formula, contract, validated priors can combined one inspectable model specification.
code: binary_priors <- create_prior_specification(   binary_contract,   baseline = 0.5 )  binary_specification <- create_model_specification(   binary_contract,   readiness_audit,   binary_priors )  binary_specification ## <gp3bayes_model_specification> ##   Family: binary ##   Formula: selected ~ condition + (1 | participant_id) + (1 | stimulus_id) ##   Readiness status: ready ##   Readiness warnings: 0 ##   Prior classes: Intercept, b, sd ##   Backend: none ##   Fit performed: FALSE

title: gp3bayes
text: backend-independent binary workflow can simulate known hierarchical data-generating processes, prepare neutral long-format data, construct restricted model specification, evaluate prior predictive plausibility. model fitted posterior draws produced.
code: binary_simulation <- simulate_hierarchical_binary_data(   n_participants = 12,   trials_per_participant = 8,   n_items = 6,   random_slope_sd = 0,   seed = 2026 )  binary_workflow_contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "binary",   outcome_col = "selected",   participant_col = "participant_id",   item_col = "item_id",   trial_col = "trial_id",   condition_col = "condition",   predictors = "trial_covariate" )  binary_prepared <- prepare_hierarchical_binary_data(   binary_simulation$data,   binary_workflow_contract,   condition_levels = c("control", "treatment"),   scale_predictors = "trial_covariate" )  binary_workflow_specification <- specify_binary_model(   binary_prepared,   baseline = 0.35 )  binary_prior_check <- check_binary_prior_predictive(   binary_workflow_specification,   draws = 100,   seed = 2027 )  binary_prior_check ## <gp3bayes_binary_prior_predictive_check> ##   Adequate: TRUE ##   Draws: 100 ##   Failed checks: 0 ##   Backend: none ##   Fit performed: FALSE

title: gp3bayes
text: translate_binary_model_to_brms() converts approved package specification fixed Bernoulli-logit brms representation without compiling fitting model. fit_binary_model() optionally runs full MCMC sampling fixed brms rstan route. Neither function accepts unrestricted formula, family, backend, algorithm, Stan extension, arbitrary backend arguments. returned fit establish convergence, posterior adequacy, causal identification, substantive validity. assessments require separate diagnostic reporting gates.
code: if (requireNamespace("brms", quietly = TRUE)) {   backend_specification <- translate_binary_model_to_brms(     binary_workflow_specification   )    backend_specification }

title: gp3bayes
text: Approved binary fits can assessed conservative numerical sampling diagnostics, posterior summaries, posterior predictive checks, prior-scale sensitivity, simulation-based recovery, structured Markdown reports. threshold pass automatic convergence posterior-adequacy claim.
code: diagnostics <- diagnose_binary_fit(binary_fit) posterior <- summarise_binary_posterior(binary_fit) predictive <- check_binary_posterior_predictive(binary_fit)

title: gp3bayes
text: duration workflow supports strictly positive, finite, uncensored durations explicit recorded unit. provides deterministic simulation, preparation, inspectable priors, prior predictive checks, restricted optional full-MCMC fitting brms rstan.
code: duration_simulation <- simulate_hierarchical_duration_data(seed = 2026) duration_contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "duration",   outcome_col = "duration",   participant_col = "participant_id",   item_col = "item_id",   trial_col = "trial_id",   condition_col = "condition",   outcome_unit = "milliseconds" ) duration_prepared <- prepare_hierarchical_duration_data(   duration_simulation$data,   duration_contract,   condition_levels = c("control", "treatment") ) duration_specification <- specify_duration_model(   duration_prepared,   baseline = 500 )

what: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
title: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
text: Audits whether data frame satisfies observable data requirements existing model contract created create_model_contract(). audit backend-independent construct formula, define executable priors, fit model, establish model adequacy.

title: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
code: audit_model_readiness(data, contract)

title: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
text: data data frame containing declared outcome, grouping identifiers, predictors, optional design columns. contract gp3bayes_model_contract created create_model_contract().

title: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
text: object class gp3bayes_readiness_audit. object records: whether data ready proceed later model-building gate; pass, warning, failure counts; one structured row per readiness check; declared observed column summaries; audited model contract. input data retained returned object.

title: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
text: readiness audit evaluates observable data properties . Passing audit establish convergence, model adequacy, predictive validity, causal identification, substantive validity. Failures block progression later model-building gate. Warnings identify weak unusual structures require review automatically block progression. Binary outcomes must logical numeric values encoded exclusively zero one, classes observed. Duration outcomes must numeric, finite, strictly positive, uncensored, variable.

title: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
text: Readiness checks determine whether model scientifically justified. Behavioural measurements must interpreted direct measures latent psychological protected attributes.

title: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
code: binary_data <- data.frame(   participant_id = rep(c("p1", "p2"), each = 4),   trial_id = rep(1:4, times = 2),   condition = rep(c("control", "treatment"), times = 4),   selected = c(0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 1, 0) )  binary_contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "binary",   outcome_col = "selected",   participant_col = "participant_id",   trial_col = "trial_id",   condition_col = "condition" )  audit_model_readiness(binary_data, binary_contract) #> <gp3bayes_readiness_audit> #>   Family: binary #>   Rows: 8 #>   Status: ready #>   Ready: TRUE #>   Checks: 17 passed, 0 warnings, 0 failures

title: Build an Approved Model Formula — build_model_formula
text: participant random intercept always included. contract$random_slope TRUE, replaced correlated participant intercept--condition-slope term. declared item identifier adds crossed item random intercept. trial identifier treated row key added predictor grouping factor. declared time column included linear population-level term .

title: Build an Approved Model Formula — build_model_formula
code: contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "binary",   outcome_col = "selected",   participant_col = "participant_id",   item_col = "stimulus_id",   condition_col = "condition",   predictors = "age_z" )  build_model_formula(contract) #> selected ~ condition + age_z + (1 | participant_id) + (1 | stimulus_id) #> <environment: base>

title: Check Binary Prior Predictive Behaviour — check_binary_prior_predictive
text: specification gp3bayes_binary_model_specification. draws Number prior predictive data sets. seed Non-negative integer random-number seed. plausible_rate Increasing lower upper limits plausible overall condition-specific event rates. boundary_probability Probability thresholds used identify prior mass close zero one. extreme_contrast Absolute probability-scale condition contrast considered extreme. maximum_degenerate_participant_fraction Maximum participant fraction allowed -zero -one replicated outcomes draw. maximum_boundary_mass Maximum fraction row probabilities allowed beyond declared boundary thresholds draw. maximum_extreme_probability Maximum acceptable fraction prior predictive draws violating criterion.

title: Check Binary Prior Predictive Behaviour — check_binary_prior_predictive
text: Failure select alter priors automatically. indicates declared priors design generate outcomes require substantive review. check assesses prior implications, posterior adequacy model fit.

title: Check Binary Prior Predictive Behaviour — check_binary_prior_predictive
code: simulation <- simulate_hierarchical_binary_data(   n_participants = 12,   trials_per_participant = 8,   seed = 2026 )  contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "binary",   outcome_col = "selected",   participant_col = "participant_id",   item_col = "item_id",   trial_col = "trial_id",   condition_col = "condition" )  prepared <- prepare_hierarchical_binary_data(   simulation$data,   contract,   condition_levels = c("control", "treatment") )  specification <- specify_binary_model(   prepared,   baseline = 0.35 )  check_binary_prior_predictive(   specification,   draws = 100,   seed = 2027 ) #> <gp3bayes_binary_prior_predictive_check> #>   Adequate: TRUE #>   Draws: 100 #>   Failed checks: 0 #>   Backend: none #>   Fit performed: FALSE

what: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
text: Creates inspectable model-contract object one two model families approved initial gp3bayes development scope. function records neutral data-column mappings preserving approved likelihood, link, estimands, assumptions, diagnostics, sensitivity requirements, interpretation boundaries.

title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
code: create_model_contract(   family,   outcome_col,   participant_col,   item_col = NULL,   trial_col = NULL,   condition_col = NULL,   time_col = NULL,   predictors = character(),   interaction = NULL,   random_slope = FALSE,   outcome_unit = NULL,   notes = character() )

title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
text: family Character scalar identifying approved model family. Supported values "binary" "duration". outcome_col Character scalar naming outcome column. participant_col Character scalar naming participant identifier column. item_col Optional character scalar naming item stimulus identifier column. trial_col Optional character scalar naming trial identifier column. condition_col Optional character scalar naming focal condition column. time_col Optional character scalar naming linear time trial order column. define time-course autocorrelation model. predictors Character vector naming additional predictors. interaction Optional character vector length two naming one prespecified two-way interaction. Higher-order multiple interactions supported initial contract. random_slope Logical scalar indicating whether one participant-level random slope focal condition requested. Readiness must assessed separately fitting. outcome_unit Optional character scalar recording outcome unit. required duration family must NULL binary family. notes Optional character vector containing user-supplied design analysis notes. Notes override approved model contract.

title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
text: object class gp3bayes_model_contract. named list containing approved methodological specification, neutral column mappings, requested model structure, assumptions, diagnostics, sensitivity requirements, limitations, unsupported uses.

title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
text: returned object specification audit record. validate data frame, construct backend formula, fit model, imply proposed analysis appropriate. gates handled separate workflows. binary contract uses Bernoulli likelihood logit link. duration contract uses lognormal likelihood strictly positive, finite, uncensored durations.

title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
text: Contract creation establish causal identification, model adequacy, convergence, predictive validity, substantive validity. Behavioural measurements must interpreted direct measures latent psychological protected attributes.

title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
code: binary_contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "binary",   outcome_col = "selected",   participant_col = "participant_id",   item_col = "stimulus_id",   trial_col = "trial_id",   condition_col = "condition" )  binary_contract #> <gp3bayes_model_contract> #>   Family: binary #>   Likelihood: Bernoulli #>   Link: logit #>   Outcome: selected #>   Participant: participant_id #>   Item: stimulus_id #>   Condition: condition #>   Random slope requested: FALSE #>   Fitting performed: FALSE  duration_contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "duration",   outcome_col = "response_time",   participant_col = "participant_id",   trial_col = "trial_id",   condition_col = "condition",   outcome_unit = "milliseconds" )  duration_contract #> <gp3bayes_model_contract> #>   Family: duration #>   Likelihood: lognormal #>   Link: identity on mean log duration #>   Outcome: response_time #>   Participant: participant_id #>   Condition: condition #>   Outcome unit: milliseconds #>   Random slope requested: FALSE #>   Fitting performed: FALSE

what: Create a Backend-Independent Prior Specification — create_prior_specification
title: Create a Backend-Independent Prior Specification — create_prior_specification
text: Creates inspectable prior table one approved model family. returned object contains backend-specific prior objects performs sampling.

title: Create a Backend-Independent Prior Specification — create_prior_specification
code: create_prior_specification(   contract,   baseline = NULL,   intercept_scale = NULL,   coefficient_scale = NULL,   group_sd_scale = 1,   residual_scale = NULL,   correlation_eta = 2,   student_df = 3 )

title: Create a Backend-Independent Prior Specification — create_prior_specification
text: contract gp3bayes_model_contract created create_model_contract(). baseline Numeric scalar describing expected baseline outcome. binary models probability strictly zero one. default 0.5. duration models strictly positive baseline median recorded outcome unit must supplied. intercept_scale Optional positive numeric scalar normal intercept prior. Defaults 1.5 binary models 1 duration models. coefficient_scale Optional positive numeric scalar normal population-level coefficient priors. Defaults 1 binary models 0.5 duration models. group_sd_scale Positive numeric scalar half-Student-t group-level standard-deviation priors. residual_scale Optional positive numeric scalar half-Student-t residual standard-deviation prior. applies duration family defaults 1. correlation_eta Numeric scalar greater equal one LKJ prior used participant-level random slope requested. student_df Positive numeric scalar giving degrees freedom half-Student-t scale priors.

title: Create a Backend-Independent Prior Specification — create_prior_specification
text: object class gp3bayes_prior_specification.

title: Create a Backend-Independent Prior Specification — create_prior_specification
text: Binary baseline probabilities transformed logit function. Duration baseline medians transformed natural logarithm.

title: Create a Backend-Independent Prior Specification — create_prior_specification
code: binary_contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "binary",   outcome_col = "selected",   participant_col = "participant_id" )  create_prior_specification(   binary_contract,   baseline = 0.35 ) #> <gp3bayes_prior_specification> #>   Family: binary #>   Baseline: 0.35 #>   Parameter classes: Intercept, b, sd #>   Backend: none #>   Executable: FALSE

what: Diagnose a Fitted Binary Model — diagnose_binary_fit
title: Diagnose a Fitted Binary Model — diagnose_binary_fit
text: Computes rank-normalized R-hat, bulk tail effective sample sizes, divergent-transition counts, maximum-treedepth saturation, chain-level energy diagnostics approved binary fit.

title: Diagnose a Fitted Binary Model — diagnose_binary_fit
code: diagnose_binary_fit(   fit,   rhat_pass = 1.01,   rhat_fail = 1.05,   ess_per_chain_pass = 100,   ess_per_chain_fail = 50,   maximum_treedepth_fraction = 0.01,   ebfmi_pass = 0.3,   ebfmi_fail = 0.2 )

title: Diagnose a Fitted Binary Model — diagnose_binary_fit
text: fit fitted gp3bayes_fit. rhat_pass R-hat value component passes. rhat_fail R-hat value component fails. ess_per_chain_pass Bulk tail ESS per chain component passes. ess_per_chain_fail Bulk tail ESS per chain component fails. maximum_treedepth_fraction Maximum fraction post-warmup draws may reach configured maximum treedepth component fails. ebfmi_pass E-BFMI value energy component passes. ebfmi_fail E-BFMI value energy component fails.

title: Diagnose a Fitted Binary Model — diagnose_binary_fit
text: gp3bayes_binary_diagnostics object containing parameter, component, chain-level diagnostic tables.

title: Diagnose a Fitted Binary Model — diagnose_binary_fit
text: overall status "fail" component fails, "review" component requires review assessed, "pass" every component passes. pass automatically establish convergence posterior adequacy.

what: gp3bayes: Contract-First Bayesian Workflows for Hierarchical Behavioural Data — gp3bayes-package
title: gp3bayes: Contract-First Bayesian Workflows for Hierarchical Behavioural Data — gp3bayes-package
text: gp3bayes provides package-neutral infrastructure transparent, contract-first Bayesian workflows repeated-measures hierarchical behavioural data. package implements model contracts, readiness audits, deterministic hierarchical binary simulation, recorded transformations, restricted model specifications, inspectable priors, prior-predictive checks, optional full-MCMC fitting approved binary models brms rstan. Fitting alone establish convergence posterior adequacy.

title: gp3bayes: Contract-First Bayesian Workflows for Hierarchical Behavioural Data — gp3bayes-package
text: initial development scope restricted : hierarchical Bernoulli-logit models binary trial-level outcomes; hierarchical lognormal models strictly positive uncensored durations. Additional outcome families require separate methodological approval.

title: gp3bayes: Contract-First Bayesian Workflows for Hierarchical Behavioural Data — gp3bayes-package
text: Core validation, contract, simulation, preparation, specification functionality remains usable without Bayesian backend. Binary model fitting uses optional brms interface fixed rstan sampling backend restricted, contract-aware functions rather unrestricted general-purpose formula wrapper.

title: gp3bayes: Contract-First Bayesian Workflows for Hierarchical Behavioural Data — gp3bayes-package
text: Behavioural measurements directly reveal emotion, stress, cognition, comprehension, personality, diagnosis, deception, intention, latent psychological states. Associations must described causal effects unless design estimand justify language.

title: Prepare Hierarchical Binary Data — prepare_hierarchical_binary_data
text: data data frame containing columns declared contract. contract binary gp3bayes_model_contract. outcome_mapping Optional named vector mapping two labelled outcome values 0 1. required non-logical, non-0/1 outcomes. condition_levels Optional two-value vector listing condition levels reference--focal order. condition_coding Two distinct finite numeric values used encode declared condition. default c(-0.5, 0.5). scale_predictors Character vector naming declared numeric predictors centre divide sample standard deviation. scale_time Whether centre scale declared linear time variable. missing Either "error" "drop". Dropping performed explicit argument selected, removed row positions recorded.

title: Prepare Hierarchical Binary Data — prepare_hierarchical_binary_data
code: simulation <- simulate_hierarchical_binary_data(   n_participants = 12,   trials_per_participant = 8,   seed = 2026 )  contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "binary",   outcome_col = "selected",   participant_col = "participant_id",   item_col = "item_id",   trial_col = "trial_id",   condition_col = "condition",   predictors = c(     "participant_covariate",     "trial_covariate"   ),   interaction = c(     "condition",     "participant_covariate"   ),   random_slope = TRUE )  prepared <- prepare_hierarchical_binary_data(   simulation$data,   contract,   condition_levels = c("control", "treatment") )  prepared #> <gp3bayes_binary_prepared> #>   Input rows: 96 #>   Analysis rows: 96 #>   Rows removed: 0 #>   Readiness: ready_with_warnings #>   Fixed matrix columns: (Intercept), condition, participant_covariate, trial_covariate, condition:participant_covariate #>   Backend: none #>   Fit performed: FALSE

code: run_binary_recovery(   repetitions = 20L,   n_participants = 30L,   trials_per_participant = 16L,   n_items = 12L,   include_items = TRUE,   random_slope = TRUE,   seed = 1001L,   chains = 4L,   iter = 1500L,   warmup = 750L,   cores = min(chains, .gp3b_default_cores(chains)),   adapt_delta = 0.95,   max_treedepth = 12L,   refresh = 0L,   interval_probability = 0.95,   minimum_repetitions = 20L,   maximum_standardized_bias = 0.25,   minimum_coverage = 0.8,   minimum_diagnostic_pass_fraction = 0.8,   continue_on_error = TRUE )

title: Run Binary Parameter Recovery — run_binary_recovery
text: repetitions Number simulation-fit repetitions. n_participants, trials_per_participant, n_items Synthetic design sizes. include_items Whether crossed item effects included. random_slope Whether participant condition slope generated fitted. seed First simulation seed. chains, iter, warmup, cores, adapt_delta, max_treedepth, refresh Restricted sampling controls. interval_probability Central posterior interval probability. minimum_repetitions Repetitions required overall pass possible. maximum_standardized_bias Maximum absolute bias divided empirical standard deviation estimates pass. minimum_coverage Minimum empirical interval coverage pass. minimum_diagnostic_pass_fraction Minimum fraction fits diagnostic pass. continue_on_error Whether failed repetitions recorded instead stopping.

title: Simulate Hierarchical Binary Data — simulate_hierarchical_binary_data
text: n_participants Number participants. trials_per_participant Number observations per participant. n_items Number crossed items include_items = TRUE. intercept Population intercept log-odds scale. condition_effect Population condition contrast log-odds scale. participant_covariate_effect Participant-covariate coefficient. trial_covariate_effect Trial-covariate coefficient. interaction_effect Condition--participant-covariate coefficient. participant_sd Participant random-intercept standard deviation. item_sd Crossed item random-intercept standard deviation. random_slope_sd Participant condition-slope standard deviation. random_slope_cor Correlation participant intercepts condition slopes. must lie strictly -1 1. condition_probability Treatment probability balanced_condition = FALSE. balanced_condition Whether participant receives approximately balanced condition sequence. include_items Whether generate crossed item identifier. seed Non-negative integer random-number seed.

title: Specify a Backend-Independent Binary Model — specify_binary_model
code: simulation <- simulate_hierarchical_binary_data(   n_participants = 12,   trials_per_participant = 8,   seed = 2026 )  contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "binary",   outcome_col = "selected",   participant_col = "participant_id",   item_col = "item_id",   trial_col = "trial_id",   condition_col = "condition" )  prepared <- prepare_hierarchical_binary_data(   simulation$data,   contract,   condition_levels = c("control", "treatment") )  specification <- specify_binary_model(   prepared,   baseline = 0.35 )  specification #> <gp3bayes_binary_model_specification> #>   Formula: selected ~ condition + (1 | participant_id) + (1 | item_id) #>   Fixed formula: selected ~ condition #>   Baseline probability: 0.35 #>   Readiness: ready_with_warnings #>   Fitting engine: none #>   Backend dependency: none #>   Fit performed: FALSE

title: Translate a Binary Model Specification to brms — translate_binary_model_to_brms
code: if (requireNamespace("brms", quietly = TRUE)) {   simulation <- simulate_hierarchical_binary_data(     n_participants = 12,     trials_per_participant = 8,     n_items = 6,     random_slope_sd = 0,     seed = 2026   )    contract <- create_model_contract(     family = "binary",     outcome_col = "selected",     participant_col = "participant_id",     item_col = "item_id",     trial_col = "trial_id",     condition_col = "condition"   )    prepared <- prepare_hierarchical_binary_data(     simulation$data,     contract,     condition_levels = c("control", "treatment")   )    specification <- specify_binary_model(     prepared,     baseline = 0.35   )    translate_binary_model_to_brms(specification) } #> <gp3bayes_binary_backend_specification> #>   Formula: selected ~ condition + (1 | participant_id) + (1 | item_id) #>   Family: Bernoulli-logit #>   Interface: brms #>   Sampling backend: rstan #>   Algorithm: sampling #>   Backend available: TRUE #>   Compiled: FALSE #>   Fit performed: FALSE

title: gp3bayes 0.0.0.9000
text: Created independent gp3bayes package scaffold. Defined initial scope contract-first Bayesian workflows hierarchical behavioural data. Restricted initial development hierarchical Bernoulli-logit hierarchical lognormal-duration model families. Added package-level documentation explicit interpretation boundaries. Added initial deterministic scope test using testthat edition 3. Added MIT licence. Added standard GitHub Actions workflows cross-platform R CMD check pkgdown deployment. Added canonical repository, issue-tracker, pkgdown website metadata. Added create_model_contract() two approved initial model families neutral column mappings explicit methodological specifications. Added concise gp3bayes_model_contract print method deterministic validation tests. Added audit_model_readiness() backend-independent assessment outcome validity, declared columns, missingness, repeated measurements, item trial structure, predictors, interactions, time terms, requested participant-level random slopes. Added structured gp3bayes_readiness_audit results explicit pass, warning, failure statuses concise print method. Added build_model_formula() deterministic, backend-independent construction approved fixed-effects, interaction, participant, item, time, optional participant-level random-slope structures. Added create_prior_specification() validate_prior_specification() explicit binary-logit lognormal-duration prior records without creating executable backend objects. Added create_model_specification() combine model contract, successful readiness audit, approved formula, validated priors one inspectable backend-independent specification. Added concise print methods deterministic validation tests formulas, priors, compatibility checks, complete specifications. Added simulate_hierarchical_binary_data() deterministic hierarchical Bernoulli-logit simulation participant effects, optional crossed item effects, optional participant condition slopes, controlled imbalance, stored true-parameter record. Added prepare_hierarchical_binary_data() explicit binary-outcome mapping, condition coding, recorded predictor scaling, missing-data decisions, readiness auditing, fixed-effects matrix construction. Added specify_binary_model() combine prepared data approved binary contract, restricted hierarchical formula, validated backend-independent prior specification. Added check_binary_prior_predictive() deterministic simulation family-specific prior predictions structured plausibility checks without fitting model requiring Bayesian backend. Added concise print methods, generated documentation, 89 focused tests backend-independent binary workflow foundation. Added repository installed-package citation metadata CITATION.cff inst/CITATION. Refined package description match currently implemented backend-independent contract, readiness, simulation, preparation, specification, prior-predictive functionality. Added restricted binary model translation approved package specifications brms Bernoulli-logit formulas priors. Added optional full-MCMC fitting fixed brms rstan sampling route without unrestricted formulas backend arguments. Added conservative fit metadata records sampling settings explicitly withholding convergence posterior-adequacy claims. Added conservative binary posterior diagnostics covering R-hat, bulk tail ESS, divergences, maximum-treedepth saturation, chain-level energy diagnostics. Added posterior summaries, binary posterior predictive checks, prior-scale sensitivity, simulation-based recovery, diagnostic plots, structured Markdown model reports. Diagnostic, predictive, sensitivity, recovery statuses never create automatic convergence, adequacy, robustness, validation claims. Added complete hierarchical lognormal duration workflow strictly positive finite uncensored outcomes. Added deterministic duration simulation, explicit unit conversion preparation, model specification, prior predictive checks, restricted brms translation, full MCMC fitting rstan. Zero, negative, censored, truncated, shifted, survival, Gamma, Weibull, mixture outcomes remain outside approved duration contract.
